subject: 🏃 Wednesday — Upper Pull (legs rest)
html:
Today: Upper Pull only. No running, no lower body. This is a scheduled legs-rest day — the knee went Yellow Monday afternoon and the plan gives it through Wednesday to fully settle before Thursday's easy 5 mi. Keep the session to ~25 min, moderate effort, nothing that leaves upper DOMS bad enough to wreck run form tomorrow.

Dynamic warm-up (~5 min, rep-based): 10 arm circles each direction, 10 shoulder rolls forward/back, 10 scapular retractions, 10 cat-cows, 10 band pull-aparts.

Upper Pull — 3 rounds:

ExerciseWeightSets × Reps
Pull-ups (or lat pulldown)Bodyweight / 85 lb pulldown3×8–10
Dumbbell row (single arm)30 lbs/hand3×10 each
Face pullsLight band / 20 lb cable3×15
Bicep curls15 lbs/hand3×12
Band pull-apartsLight band3×15

Focus cue: Squeeze the shoulder blades together and control the eccentric on every rep. Pulling work is posture work — good posture at mile 20 is worth more than any bicep PR. Face pulls especially: light weight, slow, never grind.

Context: Week 1 of 15, Base phase, 14 weeks to race day. The entire job of Weeks 1–2 is confirming the knee tolerates consistent load before tempo starts Week 3 — not building fitness. You have not run since Saturday in Cambria; this is fine and by design.

Adaptation notes: Tuesday's run rest was the right call given Monday's Yellow. Same rule applies tonight: if the knee is clean by Wednesday evening (no tingling, no awareness on stairs), Thursday's easy 5 mi is on at 9:15–10:00/mi pace. If it's still aware, we cut Thursday to 3 mi or slide it to Friday. Report the knee status either way so I can adjust.

Get after it.
